name: Walnuts
description: Walnuts (Juglans spp.) are not directly toxic to cats in the same way as to dogs, but they present several health risks. The primary concern is juglone — a compound in black walnut that causes toxicity — and the risk of mould contamination with tremorgenic mycotoxins in any walnut that has been stored or is past its freshness.
safetyNotes: Juglone risk (Black Walnut): juglone causes GI and neurological toxicity in cats; black walnuts are more dangerous than English walnuts
Mycotoxin risk: mouldy walnuts contain tremorgenic mycotoxins — causes severe tremors, seizures, and ataxia within hours
Physical hazard: walnut shells are hard and brittle; sharp fragments can injure the mouth and GI tract
High fat content: large amounts may trigger pancreatitis
If your cat ingests a mouldy walnut or shows tremors after contact with walnuts, seek veterinary care immediately

symptoms.name: If a cat ingests walnuts, the primary risks arise from the physical properties of the nut and its high fat and fiber content
Gastrointestinal Upset
symptoms.onset: Within a few hours of ingestion.
symptoms.notes: The high fiber content and heavy amount of oils in nuts make them very difficult for cats to digest, easily leading to acute digestive distress

symptoms.name: Pancreatitis
symptoms.onset: 24 to 72 hours (or progressively over a few days).
symptoms.notes: Walnuts contain very high amounts of oils and fats, which can trigger a painful inflammation of the pancreas known as pancreatitis1.... The clinical signs of pancreatitis in cats are often vague, subtle, and non-specific; the most common signs are profound lethargy, partial or complete anorexia (los

symptoms.name: Choking or Intestinal Obstruction
symptoms.onset: Immediate (for choking) or progressive over hours to days (for intestinal blockage).
symptoms.notes: Because nuts are hard and difficult for a cat to digest, intact walnuts or large pieces pose a significant physical hazard. They can become lodged in the throat as a choking hazard or cause dangerous physical blockages within the intestinal tract
